source: Invest Like the Best (YouTube) — Watts and wafers
summary: Meta is in a better competitive position than a year ago due to Zuckerberg's AI-first transformation and the impressive Muse model, though still behind Google in absolute terms
summary_extended: Baker argues that Meta has meaningfully improved its competitive position in AI over the past year, crediting Zuckerberg for making Meta the only true internet giant to fully commit to an AI-first internal transformation, including billion-dollar talent contracts to attract top researchers. He points to Meta's Muse model (described as Meta's first multimodal sequence learning model) as concrete evidence of this progress — while not yet on the Pareto frontier of frontier models, Baker found its proximity to that frontier 'pretty impressive' given where Meta stood previously. Baker's core framework is that rates of change matter more than absolute levels over 3-year investment horizons, meaning Meta's trajectory is more important than the fact that it still trails Google in absolute AI capability terms. The investment implication is that META is a compelling medium-term holding as its AI-first transformation compounds, even if it is not yet the outright AI leader among internet giants.
detail: Baker gives Zuckerberg significant credit for making Meta an AI-first company internally — the only true internet giant to have done so. He credits the billion-dollar talent contracts. Muse (Meta's first MSL model) is not on the Pareto frontier but is 'pretty close,' which Baker found very impressive. Rates of change matter more than levels over 3-year timeframes.
confidence_evidence: Baker provides specific model quality assessment (Muse near but not on Pareto frontier) and frames Meta's trajectory positively
quote_fragment: Zuckerberg making Meta an AI first company — only true internet giant to have done that

ticker_audit.reasoning: The entire thesis is specifically about Meta's AI transformation, Zuckerberg's leadership decisions, and the Muse model — Meta is the direct subject of this investment view.
